title: IAME_Envelope
description: Internal (Inter-Agent Message) Envelope. Verified against XSI-AIMS v2.1.2. Every authority-bearing message inside the seal is structured this way and is checkable before a byte is acted on.

cryptographic_domain.description: Must equal 'internal'. An internal envelope routed across the perimeter to the external domain is rejected as a domain-boundary violation; internal and external signing domains never share key material.

signature_suite.description: Internal per-message signing FLOOR is the hybrid Ed25519 + ML-DSA-65 construction (secure as long as either half holds). Pure ML-DSA is not a conformant internal option before the post-2031 transition; the more conservative post-quantum scheme (SLH-DSA) is reserved for the EXTERNAL domain's algorithm-agility, never the internal envelope.

key_epoch.description: Monotonic key epoch. A message signed under a superseded epoch is fenced (rejected); on rotation, old key material is zeroized.

path_id.description: One of the 22 canonical paths. A Temporary Path (a gated, bounded-lifetime Architect-created extension) is not a canonical PATH-NN and is registered separately.

message_type.description: Per the specification's message-type vocabulary. Witnessing is a message-type PROPERTY carried by OBSERVATION / ALERT traffic on any canonical path — there is no 'WITNESS' message type and no dedicated witness path class (the former W-paths were retired). Streamed output uses STREAM_CHUNK / STREAM_FINALIZE / STREAM_ABORT sub-types on a REPORT/OBSERVATION-class output; those are an output-delivery discipline, not authority-bearing message types.

time_to_live.description: An expired TTL is a SOFT (stale, non-malicious) rejection and does not incur a catastrophic trust penalty — distinct from a signature/authority failure, which is a live safety event.

auxiliary_authority.description: Conditional block, present only when the message authorizes invoking an external Auxiliary. Absent for ordinary internal traffic.

previous_chain_hash.description: Hash-linked, append-only tamper-evidence (a Merkle/Certificate-Transparency-style chain with signed checkpoints and timeline entanglement) — not a blockchain and not consensus-mined outside Cluster Mode.
